results.title: CreateOS Sandbox | Firecracker micro-VMs for AI agents
results.content:
```
import { CreateosSandboxClient } from "@nodeops-createos/sandbox"; // baseUrl from CREATEOS_SANDBOX_BASE_URL, apiKey from CREATEOS_SANDBOX_API_KEY const client = new CreateosSandboxClient(); const sandbox = await client.createSandbox({ shape: "s-4vcpu-4gb", rootfs: "devbox:1", }); try { const { result } = await sandbox.runCommand("node", ["--version"]); console.log(result.stdout); // "v20.x.x" } finally { // sandboxes bill while running, so always tear down await sandbox.destroy(); }
```

SDK on GitHubCLI on GitHubBrowse examplesRead the docs

## A Real Machine Boundary, Not a Shared Kernel.

Every sandbox is a Firecracker micro-VM running its own Linux kernel. That is the gating difference: the isolation is at the level of the machine, not a process namespace you hope holds. [...] Is CreateOS Sandbox production-ready?

CreateOS Sandbox is in alpha. The architecture is real and working: per-VM guest kernel, eBPF egress, sandbox networking, fork, and self-host. Audit logging, RBAC, and SOC 2, HIPAA, GDPR, and ISO certifications are on the roadmap, not yet held, with self-host as the interim control. We do not publish latency numbers we have not measured.

## Give your agents a computer of their own.

Grab 500 free credits and spin up your first sandbox, or book a design-partner call.

Grab 500 free creditsBook a design-partner callView pricing [...] CreateOS

Human view

Book a demo

# Give Every Agent Its Own Computer.

CreateOS Sandbox runs each workload in a Firecracker micro-VM with its own kernel. Connect sandboxes over a private network, pause to zero with state preserved, and never pay an egress fee. Drive it from a TypeScript SDK, the CLI, or MCP.

Grab 500 free creditsBook a design-partner callView on GitHubRead the docsView pricing

## Spin up a Sandbox in Seconds.

Install the SDK, then drive sandboxes from TypeScript, the CLI, or MCP, so an agent can provision its own compute through a tool call. Spawn it, run a command, read the output, tear it down.

```
npm install @nodeops-createos/sandbox # or: bun add @nodeops-createos/sandbox
```

results.title: Is Windows Sandbox completely broken after the 24H2 update for anyone else?
results.content:
- I managed to figure out this internet access/DNS issue within Windows Sandbox on 24H2 that can be worked around by configuring 8.8.8.8 as a DNS server. Turns out that if your org have configured a firewall policy that disables local rule merging on Public profile like below, then native firewall rules created automatically by Windows specifically to allow DNS traffic between Sandbox and host get blocked off. The names of those rules begin with "HNS Container Networking", they are visible through local Firewall console. To resolve you either set up a rule to allow inbound udp/53 on Public profile through Intune/GPO, or enable rule merging. P.S. Prior to 24H2 you could just set your main network profile to Private and Sandbox would follow. On 24H2 Sandbox sticks to Public regardless. [...] - Hey all After the windows updates January for W11 24H2 I can no longer access internet from Sandbox. It is crucial to my work role and I am trying desperately to get it fixed. Any tips?
- I have a similar issue. It works first time then just hangs on the next launch with CPU stuck at 100% usage. Stopping the Container Manager service is the only thing that brings it back down again. Uninstalling then re-installing Sandbox fixes it for short time, but the fault just keeps coming back.
- I thought it's just me. (There is still a chance that my problem doesn't have the same root cause as yours.) Sorry, I don't have a fix yet. Please let me know if you discovered one. [...] This feature has never been terribly reliable in any Windows version (random disconnects, crashes etc) but it definitely seems worse on W11 24H2, to the point of being unusable. At this time, Sandbox only works once per install/uninstall. That is, I can only reliably launch it once after enabling the feature. After that, it seems to launch (splash screen appears for a few seconds) and then closes. Trying to launch it again, I get notified that I can only run one instance of sandbox at a time. Restarting does not seem to help, which seems quite peculiar. In order to get it to run once again, I have to disable Sandbox in Windows Features and reenable it - which takes about 20 minutes for some reason, on a laptop with an i9 13905 and 64gigs of RAM (it was very fast in older Windows

results.content:
OWNERS
FAQs: Wallet
FAQs: SAND
FAQs: Marketplace
FAQs: Staking
FAQs: LAND
FAQs: Experience Publishing
FAQs: The Sandbox Bridge

## Need More Help?

If you have checked the FAQs, but still have more questions about The Sandbox, feel free to reach out to us via the following options (please choose only one).

Response times for support varies based on the current size of the support queues.

🎫 Web Support

Log in and open a support ticket at The Sandbox Support Portal.

✨ Creator Forum Support

The Sandbox Forum is a place for creators to find and share support and resources, get news, suggest features, etc.

📧 Email Support

Send an email to our Support team at [[email protected]](/cdn-cgi/l/email-protection#05767075756a77714556646b61676a7d2b42646860).

⚡ Discord Discussion [...] ## Other Contacts

🤝 Partnerships & Mutual Collaborations

Please review the partnerships page on The Sandbox's website, where you will find a contact form.

💬 Community Matters

For matters relating to the community, such as community ban appeals, mutual collaboration queries, complaints about staff or Ambassadors, and so on, contact our support team email:

[[email protected]](/cdn-cgi/l/email-protection#b9caccc9c9d6cbcdf9ead8d7dddbd6c197fed8d4dc)

Our support agents will refer your case to our Global Community Managers.

👮 Game & Account Ban Appeals

To submit a ban appeal for game bans, reward suspensions, leaderboard suspensions, and account bans, submit a ban appeal ticket via our support website.
Not to be used for community ban appeals, see "Community Matters".

## Policies [...] Learn more

👮 Reporting ToU violations or fraud

To report a potentially bad actor operating at The Sandbox, please send an email to our Security & Anti-Fraud team at [[email protected]](/cdn-cgi/l/email-protection#423027322d30360231232c26202d3a6c25232f27).
To report bad actors in the Discord community, please alert moderators in the community.

🎫 Account and Data Deletion

To request an account or data deletion, submit an Account & Personal Data Permanent Removal Request via The Sandbox Support Portal.

Note: We do not store any KYC documents at The Sandbox. KYC data removal requests must be sent directly to [[email protected]](/cdn-cgi/l/email-protection#dfafadb6a9bebca69faca6b1beafacf1b6b0).
Learn more
